title: First Income Reality
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you balance freedom and responsibility when you first earn money?
Which priorities should you protect before spending on wants?

stages.prompt: You just received your stipend/salary. What does this income represent?

options.label: Free money to spend however I like
options.reflection: While it might feel like free money, it's important to remember that income comes from work and should be managed responsibly.

options.label: A responsibility to manage wisely
options.reflection: Exactly! Treating income as a responsibility helps build good financial habits for life.

options.label: An opportunity to invest in my future
options.reflection: Smart thinking! Every income is an opportunity to build wealth through investing and saving.

options.label: Money for luxury and status items
options.reflection: Focusing on luxury items can lead to poor financial habits and debt accumulation.

stages.prompt: Do you treat this income as only for wants or for building a habit?

options.label: Treat it as the first step to a savings habit
options.reflection: Great approach! Building a savings habit early sets the foundation for long-term financial security.

options.label: I earned it - buy whatever I feel like
options.reflection: While it's understandable to want to enjoy your earnings, impulsive spending can lead to financial stress.

options.label: Celebrate first, then think about saving
options.reflection: While celebrating is nice, it's important to prioritize financial planning before indulgence.

options.label: Ignore the importance of financial planning
options.reflection: Ignoring financial planning can lead to unpreparedness for future needs and emergencies.

stages.prompt: Which plan makes the most sense on payday?

options.label: Spend all to celebrate and worry later
options.reflection: While celebrations are important, spending everything can lead to financial difficulties.

options.label: Spend randomly without any planning
options.reflection: Random spending without planning can lead to overspending and financial stress.

options.label: Pay off debts first, then plan spending
options.reflection: Paying off debts is important, but having a balanced plan for needs and savings is also crucial.

options.label: Cover needs, automate savings, then allocate fun money
options.reflection: Perfect! This approach ensures your needs are met while building financial security.

stages.prompt: A family or school deadline coincides with a minor want. What do you choose?

options.label: Give in now - stress makes you value the treat
options.reflection: While treats can provide temporary relief, responsibilities should take priority.

options.label: Try to do both simultaneously
options.reflection: Attempting to do both can lead to poor performance in both areas and increased stress.

options.label: Handle responsibilities first, postpone the want
options.reflection: Excellent! Prioritizing responsibilities over wants shows financial maturity.

options.label: Avoid both and do something else
options.reflection: Avoiding responsibilities can lead to bigger problems later.

stages.prompt: Which reminder keeps this first income meaningful?

options.label: Keep a log of spending and review weekly
options.reflection: Perfect! Tracking spending builds awareness and helps prevent surprise expenses.

options.label: Spend without thinking - you deserve it
options.reflection: While you do deserve rewards, mindful spending prevents future financial stress.

options.label: Compare my spending to others
options.reflection: Comparing spending to others can lead to unnecessary purchases and financial pressure.

options.label: Make impulse purchases regularly
options.reflection: Regular impulse purchases can quickly deplete your income and build poor financial habits.
